meta.setting: Jefferson State frontier, opening around French Gulch, Clear Creek, Shasta, and the Klamath mountains
meta.rdr_clone_target: RDR-clone western: horseback travel, camp life, hunting, debt, law pressure, outlaw choice, and a hard 1899 frontier tone

research_grounding.title: NPS Whiskeytown History and Culture
research_grounding.use: Mining communities, road pressure, immigrant settlement, and Clear Creek-era social conflict.

research_grounding.title: California State Parks Shasta State Historic Park
research_grounding.use: Courthouse-town material, iron shutters, brick ruins, commerce, and northern California mining-town texture.

image_prompt.subject: a small posse gathered under a hotel porch in French Gulch while rain slants across a lantern-lit street and an anxious horse paws mud
image_prompt.scene: nightfall in 1899; a buckboard stands half-loaded, upstairs silhouettes watch through curtains, unreadable handbills curl on a post, pine-black hills close behind town, and a wet road leaves the porch into darkness
image_prompt.composition: square scene plate with the porch as a sheltering frame and the road beyond as the threat line; one rider stands just outside the lantern circle to make the errand feel personal
image_prompt.camera: chest-height over-the-shoulder view from just behind a soaked rider, cinematic but still game-like, with early-HD silhouettes and baked shadow under the porch roof
material_cues: kerosene lantern glow on wet porch planks
muddy boot prints crossing old boards
creased paper tucked under a gloved thumb
patched canvas on a buckboard load
dull gunmetal kept low and practical
image_prompt.hand_cut_note: Do not make the prologue grand. Make it feel like a bad errand accepted because the room got quiet and nobody else would ride.

style_modifiers.medium: PS3-era open-world western game art, early-HD promotional render mixed with in-engine screenshot staging
render_traits: modest texture resolution on wood, cloth, mud, and leather
hard ambient occlusion under porch rails, hat brims, wagon boards, and horse legs
lantern-lit night palette with visible rain streaks
grounded mood with no theatrical hero pose
style_modifiers.palette: lantern amber, rain-black pine, muddy umber, faded blue wool, oxidized iron, cold gray sky, and weak window gold.

text_rendering.instructions: Avoid readable words. Handbills and notes should be visible only as blurred period paper shapes.

negative_prompt: copy of commercial game characters, missions, towns, or logos

self_check: Reads as PS3-era western game art at square page-card scale.
The porch, road, horse, and riders are all fully readable.
Every visible prop, garment, animal, and building belongs plausibly to 1899 frontier life.
The mood is practical and tense rather than theatrical.
No copied game characters, no readable modern text, and no inherited non-western symbols.
